name: One Piece
description: Dreams, freedom, and the bonds of a crew — unforgettable lines from One Piece.
author: Quips Editorial

quotes.content: I'm gonna be King of the Pirates!
quotes.notes: Luffy's lifelong declaration, first made in ch. 1 and repeated across the series. Viz manga renders it 'I'm going to be King of the Pirates!'; the Funimation dub uses 'I'm gonna be King of the Pirates!' — the most widely-used English wording.

quotes.content: When do you think people die? When they are shot through the heart by the bullet of a pistol? No. When they are ravaged by an incurable disease? No. When they drink a soup made from a poisonous mushroom!? No! It's when… they are forgotten.
quotes.notes: Hiluluk's dying words in the Drum Island arc, on legacy and memory. Wording follows the widely-used Viz manga rendering; the Funimation dub condenses it to 'A man dies when people forget him.'

quotes.content: Nothing happened.
quotes.notes: Thriller Bark; Zoro's reply after secretly absorbing all of Luffy's pain from Kuma. Matches the Viz manga (anime ep. 377).

quotes.content: I want to live!
quotes.notes: Enies Lobby; Robin's plea after the crew declares war on the World Government to save her. Matches the Viz manga (anime ep. 278).

quotes.content: Thank you… for loving me!
quotes.notes: Ace's dying words to Luffy and the Whitebeard Pirates at Marineford. Widely-used Viz manga rendering (anime ep. 483).

quotes.content: I don't want to conquer anything. I just think the guy with the most freedom in this whole ocean… is the Pirate King!
quotes.notes: Luffy to Rayleigh at Sabaody, defining what King of the Pirates means to him. Widely-used Viz manga rendering (anime ep. 401).

quotes.content: If I can't even protect my captain's dream, then whatever ambition I have is nothing but talk!
quotes.notes: Zoro to Kuma at Thriller Bark, offering his own life for Luffy. Widely-used Viz manga rendering (anime ep. 377).

quotes.content: Luffy… help me.
quotes.notes: Arlong Park; Nami's plea at her lowest point. Consistent across the Funimation dub, official subtitles, and Viz manga (ch. 93).

quotes.content: One Piece is real!
quotes.notes: Whitebeard's dying declaration at Marineford, confirming the treasure exists and launching the countdown to the new age. Appears in Viz manga ch. 576 and anime ep. 485–486; rendered 'One Piece… is real!' / 'The One Piece is real!' across official English releases.

quotes.content: My treasure? If you want it, you can have it. Search for it — I left everything I gathered in one place!
quotes.notes: Roger's execution words that ignited the Great Pirate Era, shown in ch. 1 and the Loguetown flashback (ch. 100). Wording follows the widely-used Viz manga rendering; official English releases vary slightly in phrasing.

quotes.content: No cook would ever refuse to feed a starving person.
quotes.notes: Baratie arc; Sanji feeds his enemy Gin, stating the cook's creed born of nearly starving as a boy. Widely-used Viz manga rendering (anime ep. 20).

quotes.content: You can throw a drink in my face, you can throw food at me — I can even laugh that off. But no matter the reason, I won't forgive anyone who hurts my friend!
quotes.notes: Shanks to the bandit Higuma in Luffy's home village, establishing his loyalty to friends. Wording follows the widely-used Viz manga rendering; the Funimation dub (anime ep. 4) phrases it 'If you hurt a friend of mine, you'll pay for it regardless of your reason.'

quotes.content: I'm going to become a brave warrior of the sea!
quotes.source: One Piece, manga ch. 41 (Syrup Village arc, 1998)
quotes.notes: Usopp's recurring stated dream, echoing his father Yasopp, first voiced in the Syrup Village arc. 'Brave warrior of the sea' is the standard Viz manga / official English rendering of his goal.

quotes.content: They may not share my blood, but they are my sons. Anyone who harms them is my enemy.
quotes.notes: Captures Whitebeard's found-family sentiment for his pirates, but this exact phrasing is a paraphrase — not a documented official English rendering — and no specific chapter matches it (wording axis).

quotes.content: Don't just add up everything you've lost! Ask yourself… what is it that you still have!
quotes.notes: Jinbe stopping Luffy from despair after Ace's death at Marineford. Widely-used Viz manga rendering (anime ep. 483).

quotes.content: The only thing I regret is that I won't get to see how your dreams turn out.
quotes.notes: Ace to Luffy in his final moments at Marineford. Widely-used Viz manga rendering (anime ep. 483).

quotes.content: Until the day I defeat him and become the world's greatest swordsman, I'll never lose again!
quotes.notes: Zoro's oath to Luffy after his loss to Mihawk at the Baratie, in the chapter titled 'The Oath.' Widely-used Viz manga rendering (anime ep. 24).

quotes.content: Being alone is more painful than getting hurt.
quotes.notes: Widely circulated as a Luffy line about loneliness, but I could not confirm the exact wording appears in a specific official English chapter or episode (source axis).

quotes.content: This arm? I bet it on the new era.
quotes.notes: Shanks on the arm he lost saving young Luffy, framing it as a wager on the coming generation. Widely-documented official English rendering; delivered within the Marineford arc (exact chapter within the arc).

quotes.content: I would rather die than raise a hand against a woman.
quotes.notes: Sanji's chivalric code never to strike a woman is genuine and recurring in the official release, but this specific sentence is a paraphrase rather than a documented official English line (wording axis).

quotes.content: Take me with you… out to sea!
quotes.notes: Enies Lobby; Robin asking the Straw Hats to bring her back out to sea with them. Widely-used Viz manga rendering (anime ep. 278).

quotes.content: I can't use a sword, I can't navigate, I can't cook, and I can't even lie! I know I can't survive without the help of others!
quotes.source: One Piece, manga ch. 432 (Water 7 / Post-Enies Lobby arc, 2006)
quotes.notes: Luffy admitting how completely he relies on his crew, during the fallout with Usopp. Widely-used Viz manga rendering.

quotes.content: If you don't take risks, you can't create a future!
quotes.source: One Piece, manga ch. 879 (Whole Cake Island arc, 2017)
quotes.notes: Luffy during the escape from Whole Cake Island. Widely-documented official English rendering of the line.

quotes.content: Ace… it was fine for you to have been born!
quotes.notes: The Marineford scene is correct and in the official release, but the affirmation that it was fine for Ace to be born is Ace's own dying realization, voiced collectively by his comrades — not a distinct Whitebeard line, so the attribution is off (attribution axis).

quotes.content: Inherited will, the tide of the times, people's dreams — these are things that cannot be stopped.
quotes.notes: Roger's philosophy of inherited will, which appears in the official release as narration closing the Loguetown arc and is canonically attributed to Roger (later spoken by him to Whitebeard in the ch. 0 flashback). Widely-used Viz manga wording.

quotes.content: I'm entrusting you with this hat. It's my favorite. Give it back to me someday, when you've become a great pirate.
quotes.notes: Shanks entrusting his straw hat to young Luffy as they part ways. Widely-used Viz manga rendering (anime ep. 4).
